# terraform-module-gcp-cloudsql-database

 [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-cloudsql-database.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-cloudsql-database/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-cloudsql-database.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-cloudsql-database/commits)


Terraform module that provisions a Google Cloud SQL database instance (PostgreSQL, MySQL, or SQL Server) with Secret Manager credential storage and optional Hoop connection output.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration. 
[<img align="right" title="Share via Email" width="24" height="24" src="https://docs.cloudops.works/images/ionicons/ios-mail.svg"/>][share_email]

[<img align="right" title="Share on X" width="24" height="24" src="https://docs.cloudops.works/images/ionicons/logo-twitter.svg" />][share_twitter]


It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This module is the GCP equivalent of `terraform-module-aws-rds-database`. Unlike AWS RDS which uses separate resource
types per engine, Google Cloud SQL uses a single resource type — `google_sql_database_instance` — for all three
database engines. The engine is selected via the `database_version` field (e.g., `POSTGRES_16`, `MYSQL_8_0`,
`SQLSERVER_2022_STANDARD`).

The module provisions:
- A `google_sql_database_instance` with configurable tier, availability, disk, backup, maintenance, query insights,
  and network settings.
- An initial database (`google_sql_database`) on the instance.
- A random admin password and `google_sql_user` (unless `managed_password` is enabled).
- A GCP Secret Manager secret containing the full credentials JSON (host, port, username, password, dbname, engine,
  sslmode, connection_name).
- Optional per-field Hoop secrets and a structured `hoop_connections` output for enterprise Hoop agent integration.

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-cloudsql-database/releases).


```hcl
# terragrunt.hcl
terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-gcp-cloudsql-database.git//?ref=v1.0.0"
}

include "root" {
  path = find_in_parent_folders()
}

locals {
  local_vars = yamldecode(file("inputs.yaml"))
}

inputs = merge(
  local.local_vars,
  {
    org        = local.org
    is_hub     = local.is_hub
    spoke_def  = local.spoke_def
    extra_tags = local.extra_tags
  }
)
```

**inputs.yaml** (see `.boilerplate/inputs.yaml` for the full annotated template):
```yaml
settings:
  name_prefix: "mydb"
  database_version: "POSTGRES_16"
  tier: "db-custom-2-7680"
  availability_type: "REGIONAL"
  disk_size: 50
  backup:
    enabled: true
    point_in_time_recovery: true
  private_ip:
    enabled: true
  hoop:
    enabled: true
    community: false
    agent_id: "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx"

network:
  network_id: "projects/my-project/global/networks/my-vpc"
  allocated_ip_range: "google-managed-services-my-vpc"
```

## Quick Start

1. Ensure `make init/gcp` has been run in the module root to copy GCP boilerplate files.
2. Copy `.boilerplate/inputs.yaml` to your Terragrunt stack directory and fill in the required fields
   (`settings.database_version`, `settings.tier`, `settings.name_prefix`).
3. Run `terragrunt init && terragrunt plan` to preview the resources.
4. Run `terragrunt apply` to provision the Cloud SQL instance.


## Examples

**PostgreSQL 16 — private IP, HA, backups enabled:**
```yaml
settings:
  name_prefix: "app-postgres"
  database_version: "POSTGRES_16"
  tier: "db-custom-4-15360"
  availability_type: "REGIONAL"
  disk_size: 100
  disk_autoresize: true
  backup:
    enabled: true
    start_time: "02:00"
    point_in_time_recovery: true
    retained_backups: 14
  private_ip:
    enabled: true
    require_ssl: true

network:
  network_id: "projects/my-project/global/networks/default"
  allocated_ip_range: "google-managed-services-default"
```

**MySQL 8.0 — public IP with authorized networks:**
```yaml
settings:
  name_prefix: "app-mysql"
  database_version: "MYSQL_8_0"
  tier: "db-n1-standard-2"
  availability_type: "ZONAL"
  disk_size: 20

network:
  authorized_networks:
    - name: "office"
      cidr: "203.0.113.0/24"
```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_network"></a> [network](#input\_network) | Network configuration for Cloud SQL instance. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_settings"></a> [settings](#input\_settings) | Settings for Cloud SQL instance — see inline docs for full YAML structure. | `any` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |

## Outputs

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| <a name="output_connection_name"></a> [connection\_name](#output\_connection\_name) | The connection name of the Cloud SQL instance (project:region:instance). |
| <a name="output_credentials_secret_id"></a> [credentials\_secret\_id](#output\_credentials\_secret\_id) | The GCP Secret Manager secret ID storing the master credentials JSON. |
| <a name="output_database_name"></a> [database\_name](#output\_database\_name) | The initial database name created on the instance. |
| <a name="output_database_version"></a> [database\_version](#output\_database\_version) | The database version of the Cloud SQL instance. |
| <a name="output_hoop_connections"></a> [hoop\_connections](#output\_hoop\_connections) | Hoop connection definitions. Enterprise mode only (GCP Secret Manager has no sub-key access). Community mode returns null. |
| <a name="output_instance_id"></a> [instance\_id](#output\_instance\_id) | The ID of the Cloud SQL instance. |
| <a name="output_instance_name"></a> [instance\_name](#output\_instance\_name) | The name of the Cloud SQL instance. |
| <a name="output_ip_address"></a> [ip\_address](#output\_ip\_address) | The first IPv4 address of the Cloud SQL instance. |
| <a name="output_master_username"></a> [master\_username](#output\_master\_username) | The master/admin username for the instance. |
| <a name="output_private_ip_address"></a> [private\_ip\_address](#output\_private\_ip\_address) | The private IP address of the Cloud SQL instance. |
